Title: Enzymatic epimerization of monoterpene indole alkaloids in kratom

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450, CYP71 family; spirooxindole alkaloid synthase
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts 3R monoterpene indole alkaloids into downstream spirooxindole alkaloids, expanding spirooxindole alkaloid diversity in kratom.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Spirooxindole alkaloids such as corynoxines, rhynchophyllines, mitragynine oxindoles, speciogynine oxindoles, pteropodines/mitraphyllines-related products
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmacologically active natural products; includes compounds with reported medicinal relevance such as opioid-related activity and potential neuroactive effects.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stems and roots in kratom accumulate greater amounts of 3R-MIAs and spirooxindole alkaloids.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in kratom; discussion notes MsSAS is expressed at higher levels in stem than in other pathway genes.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves for pathway assays and reconstitution.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agroinfiltration/transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; substrate feeding assays.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; in planta cascade assays; full pathway reconstruction; LC-MS/MS comparison with authentic standards.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsSAS accepts a broad range of 3R corynanthe- and heteroyohimbine-type MIAs. 2. It converts speciociliatine and mitraciliatine into methoxylated spirooxindole products. 3. It is inactive on tested yohimbine-type alkaloids, indicating scaffold selectivity. 4. Together with MsCO1 and MsDCR1, it enables full reconstruction of spirooxindole alkaloid biosynthesis from tryptamine in N. benthamiana.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also referred to as Ms3eCIS in prior work; identified previously and functionally expanded here rather than discovered de novo in this study.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for biocatalytic production of pharmacologically active spirooxindole alkaloids when combined with epimerization enzymes; suitable for pathway engineering stacks with MsCO1 and MsDCR1.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of kratom tissues; GenBank BioProject PRJNA1244102 (study-wide).

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BBE-like FAD-linked oxidase; berberine bridge enzyme-like protein
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpene indole alkaloid biosynthesis; 3S-to-3R epimerization branch leading to 3R-MIAs and spirooxindole alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for entry into 3R-MIA epimerization branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes (20S)-corynantheidine to the iminium intermediate (20S)-3-dehydrocorynantheidine (DHC); also oxidizes several related 3S-MIA scaffolds to corresponding iminium intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: (20S)-corynantheidine; also acts on several related corynanthe-, heteroyohimbine-, and yohimbine-type MIAs
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate iminium intermediate required for downstream reduction to 3R-MIAs and subsequent spirooxindole alkaloid formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables epimerization branch by generating the iminium intermediate from 3S-MIAs, thereby promoting formation of 3R-MIAs and downstream spirooxindole alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: 3R monoterpene indole alkaloids and downstream spirooxindole alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive alkaloids with medicinal relevance, including opioid-related and neuroactive compounds.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: 3R-MIAs and spirooxindole alkaloids are enriched in stems and roots; DHC accumulates in young leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Coexpressed with MsEnolMT in young leaf tissue; implicated in metabolically active young leaves/stems.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves for functional assays.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Young leaf and stem tissues showed active isotopologue incorporation; agroinfiltration assays in Nicotiana benthamiana; in vitro assays with purified protein.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq coexpression; heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; purification from Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite assays; mutagenesis/docking support.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsCO1 was identified as a BBE-like oxidase that converts corynantheidine to DHC. 2. It acts with MsDCR1 to epimerize 3S-MIAs into 3R-MIAs. 3. It has broad substrate specificity across corynanthe-, heteroyohimbine-, and some yohimbine-type alkaloids. 4. It does not oxidize mitragynine detectably, indicating methoxylation constrains substrate acceptance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MsCO2 is a highly similar isoform (94% identity) with similar in planta activity, but MsCO1 was used as the main representative enzyme for detailed assays. Native Nicotiana benthamiana enzymes show weak background oxidation of 3a.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key enzyme for synthetic biology routes to 3R-MIAs and spirooxindole alkaloids; should be stacked with MsDCR1 and MsSAS for production platforms.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad activity may generate multiple alkaloid products and product mixtures in engineering systems.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Medium-chain reductase/dehydrogenase; lignan aromatic alcohol dehydrogenase-like; isoflavone reductase-like
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpene indole alkaloid biosynthesis; 3S-to-3R epimerization branch
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Reduction step of corynanthe/heteroyohimbine/yohimbine epimerization branch
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream branch-point reductive stereochemical inversion step
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step determining 3R product formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces the iminium intermediate DHC to the 3R alkaloid (20S)-isocorynantheidine; also reduces DHM to speciociliatine and acts on other related iminium intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: (20S)-isocorynantheidine and other 3R-MIAs such as speciociliatine from DHM
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms 3R-MIA products that serve as terminal bioactive alkaloids or direct substrates for MsSAS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Drives stereoselective formation of 3R-MIAs from iminium intermediates, enabling accumulation of speciociliatine-like alkaloids and downstream spirooxindoles.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: 3R monoterpene indole alkaloids and spirooxindole alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmacologically active alkaloids, including more potent μ-opioid receptor agonist speciociliatine and medicinal spirooxindoles.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: 3R-MIAs and derived spirooxindoles accumulate mainly in stems and roots.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression pattern similar to upstream pathway genes; discussion notes MsDCR1 is expressed at higher levels in stem than several other pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves for in planta assays.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Identified from Naucleeae-specific sequence similarity network and validated in Nicotiana benthamiana and in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: SSN bioinformatics; transient expression; in vitro enzymology with purified enzyme; docking; mutagenesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsDCR1 stereoselectively reduces DHC to the 3R isomer isocorynantheidine. 2. It acts with MsCO1 as the epimerase pair responsible for 3R-MIA biosynthesis. 3. It has broad substrate scope, including DHM and several related iminium intermediates. 4. A loop adjacent to the active site contributes strongly to stereoselectivity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Distinct from previously known MIA iminium reductases such as MsDCS1/CrTHAS1/CpDCS/CrDPAS; closest to isoflavone reductase-like enzymes. Loop swapping with MsDCR2 altered stereoselectivity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable for engineering production of 3R-MIAs and spirooxindoles; should be paired with MsCO1 and optionally MsSAS depending desired end product.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad substrate scope may create mixed product spectra; stereoselectivity can be sensitive to structural determinants.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream side-route/branch point on iminium intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates the iminium intermediate (20S)-3-dehydrocorynantheidine at C10 to form 10-hydroxylated DHC (15a).
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Acts on a pathway intermediate/branch-point metabolite rather than a confirmed terminal product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Demonstrates that DHC is competent for downstream enzymatic tailoring, but the in planta contribution to kratom alkaloid accumulation remains unclear.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves for functional assays.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Candidate discovered by SSN analysis and tested by transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS product matching to standard.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ms10H is a CYP81 that hydroxylates DHC rather than corynantheidine. 2. It shows narrow substrate specificity among tested kratom metabolites. 3. Its native physiological role in kratom remains unresolved because corresponding products were not observed clearly in planta.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core pathway discovery in Results, but the paper explicitly states the in planta role remains unclear.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tailoring enzyme for generating novel hydroxylated iminium/MIA derivatives in biocatalytic systems, though native pathway relevance is uncertain.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May divert flux to non-native or low-abundance branches with unclear product stability or utility.
